topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on the shared family tablet, a string of cryptic symbols and an unfamiliar username. My breath hitched in my throat. I hadn't seen this. I scrolled down, my thumb hovering over the screen as if it might explode. It was a profile picture—a blurry shot of a cityscape I didn't recognize. A knot tightened in my chest, cold and hard. I leaned closer, my knuckles white against the smooth glass. This wasn't the curated, carefully managed existence I thought I knew about my daughter. A slow, creeping sense of unease began to coil in my stomach, making my hands tremble slightly as I reread the bio.

[story 2]

I found the login details tucked inside an old, forgotten password manager file. The sheer audacity of it made my jaw slacken. My heart started hammering against my ribs, a frantic drum solo in the quiet living room. I stared at the screen, the glowing text mocking me. It was a world entirely separate from the one I thought I was guiding. A wave of intense confusion washed over me, quickly followed by a sharp, defensive tightening in my shoulders. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the rug, unable to settle, my gaze darting between the screen and the closed bedroom door.

[story 3]

When I finally managed to navigate to the profile, the stream of content hit me like a physical blow. It was vibrant, unfiltered, and utterly private. A sudden dryness coated my mouth, making it difficult to form coherent thoughts. I felt a distinct pressure building behind my eyes. I backed away from the tablet, taking a deliberate, slow step backward until my spine hit the wall. My breathing became shallow and quick. Every instinct screamed at me to close the app, to pretend this had never existed, but my eyes kept snagging on the endless scroll, trapped by a sudden, overwhelming sense of exposure.
